name: Glauca
description: A design system named for the glaucous bloom: the blue-grey-green film on leaves, waves and eyes. A pale frost field is the ground; one vivid sky-blue (dies) is the load-bearing mark. Light-first: Pruina (light) leads, Profundum (dark) answers. Ships as web/CSS, Tailwind, Typst slides and posters, an Obsidian theme, Ghostty/iTerm2 presets, an oh-my-zsh prompt, VS Code and Zed themes, and R/Python plot styles.
signature: Nothing hides behind the surface. The bloom carries the substance -- pale, even, legible -- and the sky-blue dies is the one rare mark, kept rare so a page never shouts. The working exception is the code tier, where legibility duty puts the blue on keywords and admits the leaf green. See FOUNDATIONS.md.

tiers.core: Identity hues used everywhere: saxum (dark neutrals), glaucum (the bloom tints), caelum (the blue mark), pruina (pale neutrals). These define the brand.
tiers.extended: Working hues added only for code (editors, terminal ANSI): folium (the leaf-green anchor, admitted here and in dataviz), bacca (red), viola (violet), lacus (blue), unda (cyan). They never appear on posters, slides, or the web.

terminal.note: ANSI 16-colour (dark chrome; the light terminal derives from modes.light). red, yellow, magenta derived in-register; bright green is the folium anchor; bright blue is dies itself.

accessibility.standard: WCAG 2.1 (body 4.5:1, large/UI 3:1) plus a Machado-2009 CVD pass at full severity.
accessibility.cvd: The blue mark holds under all three axes (blue is the CVD-stable hue). Measured close pairs: number/function (7.2 protan, 5.5 deutan) and function/type (5.5 tritan) -- reinforced in style, not hue: keywords bold, types italic, numbers italic, comments italic, so no close pair relies on colour alone. Run scripts/cvd_check.py for the table.
rules: Blue fills on the tint ramp use aer with dark ferrum text (dies on tint is below 3:1 in dark mode).
Blue-rare is an identity-tier rule; the code tier follows accessibility-first syntax logic with dies on keywords.
Borders are deliberately faint; lift only if visible separators are wanted.

notes: 0.1.0 founds Glauca as the light-first sibling of Try-Works: same machinery, same guarantees, inverted priority. The three anchors are placed, not decorated: dies (#007AFF) is light accent-bright, dark accent-deep, the keyword colour, the cursor, and ANSI bright blue; folium (#62BA46) is the string colour, ANSI bright green, and the dataviz green; cinis (#8C8C8C) is the dark mode's muted text exactly.

typography.note: Roles, not just sizes. IBM Plex Serif carries display and headings; IBM Plex Sans carries interface and long-form body (the clinical voice); IBM Plex Mono carries code and labels. Plex has no optical-size axis, so roles bind weight, leading, tracking, figures, and measure only.

reading.note: Body face. The sans IS the reading voice in Glauca; the serif is the display voice.

gamut.note: Wide-gamut blue for P3 displays; approximations pending an OKLab audit. sRGB hexes are the fallback.

colorspace: Hues authored in sRGB and audited in OKLCH. The glaucum tint ramp is hue-consistent (~230, desaturated) with even lightness steps; dies sits near OKLCH 0.60/0.19/258.

i18n.note: Verified to cover European Portuguese including guillemets (« »), ordinals (º ª), the travessão (—), curly quotes, and the euro (€). Set lang='pt' so localized forms and accented uppercasing apply. Body is ragged-right; no web justification.
i18n.quotes: pt uses guillemets first, curly second

dataviz.note: Categorical prioritises colour-blind discriminability over brand: Okabe-Ito. Sequential stays on the blue mark (dies ramp); diverging runs blue-copper (cool-warm, the CVD-safe axis). Magnitude scales encode value, so brand hue is free there.

shapes.name: markers paired with the categorical scale

print.note: These CMYK values are uncalibrated starting points from a naive separation. Proof against a physical swatch before a print run; the printer's RIP and ICC profile are authoritative. #007AFF sits outside coated CMYK -- expect a duller press blue or run it as a spot.
print.profile: PSO Coated v3 (FOGRA51) for coated stock; FOGRA39 where v3 is unavailable.
print.ink_limit: 300% total area coverage (FOGRA51). Keep the dark saxum and rich black under it.

rich_black.note: Cool rich black that reads like pix. Use plain K for text below ~24 pt to avoid registration fringing.

print.spot: If the brand blue must match exactly, run it as a process-blue spot (Pantone 2175 C region) and confirm on a swatch book; the naive separation above will print duller.

a11y.note: Beyond body contrast: visible focus, forced-colors support, a higher-contrast path, and a reduced-transparency path. All emitted to css/a11y.css.

performance.note: Subset to i18n.unicode-range as woff2. Fallback metrics below are PLACEHOLDERS (neutral values): recompute with fontTools once the IBM Plex TTFs are in src/fonts/, then update payload_kb.

motion.note: Motion marks a change; it does not decorate. Durations are short, eases are quiet, and prefers-reduced-motion collapses them.

brand.essence: One sky-blue mark on a frost-bloom field.

family.try-works: the dark sibling: a Moby-Dick system, sea and fire, dark-first
family.glauca: the light sibling: the glaucous bloom, blue and frost, light-first

product.audience: an academic-maker shipping web, slides, posters, code, and statistical plots in R, Python, Quarto, Typst, 11ty; clinical-clean aesthetic, light-first
product.top_gap: none at 0.1.0; surfaces inherited complete from the Try-Works machinery.
product.scope: 0.x: the public surface may still move; semver from 1.0.
